info.name: DummyJSON API - Full QA Regression Suite
info.description:
Comprehensive, professional QA regression collection covering every public resource of https://dummyjson.com — Auth, Users, Products, Carts, Todos, Posts, Comments, Quotes, Recipes, and platform Utilities (test/health, IP, HTTP status mocking, image generation, 2FA/TOTP, and custom mock API generation).

Design principles:
- Environment-agnostic: all requests use the {{baseUrl}} collection variable (defaults to https://dummyjson.com) so the suite can point at a staging mirror if needed.
- Auth handling: running 'Auth > Login' captures accessToken/refreshToken/userId into collection variables automatically; all Bearer-protected requests reference {{accessToken}}.
- Each request includes assertions for status code, response time, content-type, and response schema/business-logic where relevant.
- Negative-path requests are included alongside happy-path requests (invalid ids, bad credentials, unauthorized access, 4xx/5xx mocking) to validate error handling.
- CRUD-simulating endpoints (add/update/delete) are exercised even though DummyJSON does not persist writes; assertions validate the simulated response contract only.

Recommended run order: Auth folder first (to populate tokens), then remaining folders in any order via Collection Runner or Newman.

Verified against the live API/docs at build time:
- Unsupported verbs on read-only/filter routes (e.g. DELETE on /recipes/tag/{tag}) return 404, not 405 — assertions reflect this actual contract.
- HTTP status mocking uses the path form /http/{code}/{optional-message} and the returned HTTP status genuinely matches {code}.
- /totp is a documented alias of /2fa.
- Every resource is additionally reachable behind /auth/{resource} and requires a valid Bearer token, distinct from the public unauthenticated routes.

Run with Newman: newman run DummyJSON_API_QA_Collection.postman_collection.json -e DummyJSON_QA.postman_environment.json

This collection is designed to run inside CI/CD (see the companion GitHub Actions workflow and README in the same delivery) with an HTML report generated via newman-reporter-htmlextra. Load/performance testing (k6) and version control guidance are documented separately, since those live outside what a Postman collection file can express on its own.

exec: // Ensures baseUrl always has a sane default if the variable was cleared.
// Collection-level test script runs after every request
pm.test('No server error (5xx) unless explicitly testing error mocking', function () {

item.name: 01 - Auth
item.description: Login, session, and token-refresh flows. Run first to populate {{accessToken}} for all protected requests.

exec: pm.test('Response has accessToken and refreshToken', function () {
pm.test('Username in response matches request', function () {
pm.test('Tokens are ALSO set as cookies (per docs: credentials include)', function () {

request.description: Authenticate with valid credentials and store accessToken/refreshToken as collection variables for use by protected requests.
Negative test: invalid credentials should be rejected.
Fetch the profile of the currently authenticated user using the Bearer accessToken.
Negative test: request without a valid token should be unauthorized.
Exchange a refresh token for a new access/refresh token pair.

Every resource is also reachable behind the /auth/{resource} prefix. Functionally identical to /products, but requires a valid Bearer token; useful for verifying that authenticated and public payloads stay in sync.

item.name: Get Todos As Authenticated User - Negative (garbage token)

request.description: Negative test: the /auth/{resource} prefix must enforce auth the same as /auth/me.

item.name: 02 - Users
item.description: Full CRUD + search/filter/sort coverage for the Users resource, plus nested carts/posts/todos-by-user.

Fetch users sorted by firstName ascending.
Fetch users returning only selected fields for lighter payloads.

request.description: Alternate 'select' syntax: repeated query params instead of a comma-separated list.

item.name: Unsupported Verb On Read-Only Nested Route - Negative

request.description: Edge case: DummyJSON's read-only/filter routes are not registered for write verbs. Confirmed live behavior returns 404 (route not found) rather than 405 (method not allowed) - assert the actual contract rather than the 'textbook' expectation.
Simulate deletion of a recipe.

item.name: 10 - Utilities & Tools
item.description: Platform utility endpoints: health check, IP lookup, HTTP status mocking, placeholder image generation, TOTP/2FA, and custom mock API generator.

request.description: Simple endpoint to verify the API responds; works for any HTTP verb.
Returns the caller's IP address and user agent.

Mock/test a specific HTTP status code response (404) for negative-path testing.
Mock/test a server error status code response (500).
Generate a placeholder image of given dimensions.
Generate a placeholder image with custom background/text colors and overlay text.

item.name: Mock HTTP Status Code (418 with custom message)

request.description: Mock a status code together with the optional custom message path segment.
Mock a redirect-class status code to validate client redirect handling.
The /totp path is documented as an alias of /2fa; verify both resolve identically.
Generate a real TOTP verification code from a base32 secret.
Generate a TOTP code via POST (keeps secret out of URL/logs).
Create a custom mock endpoint returning a specific JSON body for a given HTTP method.

exec: pm.test('Custom mock echoes configured payload', function () {

request.description: Run this immediately after 'Generate Custom Mock API' in the same session - it calls back the unique URL returned by that request using the GET method configured at generation time.

item.name: 11 - Schema Validation & Security Testing
item.description: JSON-Schema (data-type) validation on core resource objects, plus SQL/NoSQL injection, XSS, and tampered-token security checks.

exec: pm.test('Response matches documented JSON schema', function () {
pm.test('All required fields have correct primitive types', function () {

request.description: Data Validation: validates the response body against a JSON Schema (types + required fields), not just that fields are present.

exec: pm.test('Response matches documented JSON schema', function () {
pm.test('All required fields have correct primitive types', function () {

request.description: Data Validation: validates the user response body against a JSON Schema.

exec: pm.test('Injection payload does NOT bypass authentication', function () {
pm.test('No database/stack trace leaked in response', function () {

request.description: Security: attempts a classic SQL-injection auth-bypass payload in the username field.

exec: pm.test('Request handled safely (200 with empty/safe results)', function () {
pm.test('No server error or leaked error details', function () {

request.description: Security: sends an injection payload through a public search parameter; must be treated as a literal string.

item.name: [Security] XSS payload in new post body

exec: pm.test('Payload echoed back as plain text (not stripped silently, not executed)', function () {

request.description: Security: submits a script-injection payload in a text field; the API should store/echo it as inert text, never execute it, and consuming frontends must escape it on render.
Security: submits an event-handler-based XSS payload; verifies the API does not sanitize/break, so the responsibility for output-encoding is clearly on the frontend.

item.name: [Security] Unauthorized access to /auth/{resource} with tampered token

request.description: Security: verifies a structurally-plausible but invalid JWT is still rejected (not just an empty/missing header).

item.name: [Security] Sensitive fields not exposed in list responses

exec: pm.test('Password field presence is a known/expected part of this demo API only', function () {
// NOTE: DummyJSON intentionally exposes a demo password field for learning purposes;
// in a real production API this assertion should instead be to.not.have.property('password');

request.description: Security: spot-checks that highly sensitive fields (plaintext password) are not casually exposed in bulk list responses beyond what's documented.
